text: Hey, good morning, everybody. Good afternoon or good evening, depending on what part of the world
you're at today. I was just reading a newspaper here at the kitchen table and I stumbled upon an
article that says countries around the world are suspending shipments to the United States.
That kind of got my attention. It goes on to say here postal services in many countries,
including Japan, Australia and Mexico, have paused or stopped delivering most packages to the United
States. This follows the end of a long-standing United States rule, known as Diminimus
exception, that would allow inexpensive parcels worth $800 or less to enter the country duty-free.
So right away, I'm starting to think that I can no longer get snacks sent to me from my daughter
who lives in Korea because they're less than $800. I don't know how this is going to play out yet,
but just kind of siphoned it all here. And then some of the statistics here, there's over 30

And then the other big number that caught my eyes, 1.36 billion packages were shipped to the United
States from other countries in 2024. So that's a lot of shipping and for all that to stop at the end
of August. And now these companies are kind of scrambling right now to update their systems to
